observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require:
OsMun to prepare and submit the Monthly Summary of SOA, including discounts and adjustments, if any, to the CAD for timely recording of transaction; and
CAD to record the monthly accruals and collections of income and related receivables to ensure accurate account balances, and to assist OsMun to reconcile the noted discrepancy between the balances of the Due from NGAs and the SOA.

We recommended and the City Major agreed to:
Instruct the CAD to assist OsMun in the reconciliation of the noted discrepancy of hospital fees charged to the PhilHealth and the summary of SOA of patients, and in assessing its collectability; and
Require OsMun in the monthly reporting of accrual of hospital fees, for the timely recording of the same in the books.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:
Instruct the concerned personnel to liquidate the year-end balance of their CAs;
Require the City Accountant to issue a certification that the previous CAs have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional CAs to ensure the liquidation of CAs; and
Enforce liquidation of all CAs within the prescribed period set forth under the pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated  February 10, 1997.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the CAD to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts and from time to time, revisit the allocation of the depreciation of the PPE accounts.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the CED to:
inquire and make clarification with the GPPB-TSO on the posting of warranty security on accepted infrastructure projects in CY 2023 and prior years considering that its effective date is one year from the date of issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance; and
compel contractors to secure the certificate of final acceptance after one year upon completion of their infrastructure projects and strictly enforce the posting of warranty security before its issuance.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the CED to:
Ensure that tarpaulin signboards were posted in all awarded infrastructure projects in the exact project locations and in the prescribed specifications;
Inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; and
Regularly update the project status or the percentage of completion on the project signboards as required under Section 3.2 of COA Circular  No. 2013-004.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the CAD to:
Reconcile the under/over remittance of tax and immediately remit in full any unremitted taxes to the BIR; and
Henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation between the monthly taxes withhold and remittance, and ensure that taxes withheld are remitted in full, within ten days following the month in which the withholding was made, to avoid unnecessary incurrence of penalties, interest and/or surcharges pursuant to RMC No. 23-2007 and Section 247 (b), Chapter I of the NIRC.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the CAD to:
Continuously reconcile the unremitted amounts recorded under Due to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, and ensure the full remittance of all outstanding mandatory contributions and employees' loan amortizations to the respective government agencies;
Ensure that all subsequent remittances are made within the reglementary period in compliance with RA Nos. 8291 and 7875, as well as HDMF Circular No. 275; and
Promptly resolve discrepancies noted in the Exception Reports generated from the eBCS through continuous coordination with the CHRD and the submission of appropriate membership updating forms to GSIS before the following month’s remittance in order to prevent the recurrence of over-and-under-remittances of GSIS contributions and loan amortizations.

We commended the City's commitment and efforts in enhancing disaster response through the implementation of DRRM programs and activities, as well as for being a recipient of the Gawad Kalasag Seal of Excellence Award. We encouraged them to continue developing and sustaining disaster response initiatives while promoting best practices in disaster management.

Nevertheless, we recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct MDDRM to:
Regularly submit to the Audit Team the monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRM Fund within the prescribed period set forth under Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Submit the status report of the PPAs funded under the Continuing Appropriation and Special Trust Fund, for verification by the Audit Team.

We commended the City for its commitment and efforts to promote gender equality through implementation of GAD PPAs and for being certified GAD LLH. We likewise encourage them to continuously undertake and sustain the implementation of its gender-responsive PPAs for the promotion and empowerment of women and gender equality, and the conduct of GAD best practices.

Nevertheless, we recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the City GFPS to:
Closely coordinate with implementing offices particularly those with unimplemented GAD PPAs to ensure that challenges encountered in previous year are considered in future proposals, enabling the full implementation of programmed GAD PPAs; and
Henceforth, submit to the concerned DILG Office the revised GBP as to any changes in the DILG-endorsed GPB or if there is a need to implement additional PPAs addressing current gender issues or GAD-related initiatives and furnish the Auditor with a copy of this notification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to require the City Assessor to:

Re-assess the 6,449 RPUs based on the sufficient documentary evidence provided by its tax payers whether these are to be retained as exempted property and to return to the Assessment Roll those properties that are taxable, for proper assessment and collection of the corresponding RPT;

Provide the Audit Team with the revised and updated List of the Real Properties Exempted from Real Property Taxes upon re-assessment together with its supporting documents; and

Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 206 and 234 of RA No. 7160 and Section 16 of the Muntinlupa Tax Code in determining the property to be exempted from RPT and strictly use the codes provided by Section 2 (D), Chapter VI of the BLGF Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations.
observations.reason:
Reassessment of the properties is still on-going.

Only the list of exempted from payment of real properties as of June 30, 2023, was submitted.

On-going reassessment of properties exempted from RPT.

observations.managementAction:
The LSB will diligently follow-up with the relevant personnel/offices and ensure efficient and effective compliance with all the PPAs outlined in the SEF budget.

The LSB already gave an authorization to the Chairperson of the LSB to augment as per LSB Resolution No. 3, s. 2023. Further, revised APP and PPMP were submitted for the changes in the annual budget in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to –

Ensure that the identified LCCAP-related PPAs are consistent with the MCCDP and NCCAP pursuant to Sections 14 and 18 of RA No. 9729 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-135

Allocate from the annual appropriations an adequate funds for the formulation, development and implementation, including training, capacity building and direct intervention, of the City’s climate change programs and plans

Include a representative from the City Treasurer’s Office as part of the MCCB as recommended in Item 3.1.1 of Section 3.0 of DILG MC No. 2014-135.
observations.managementAction:
The Management agreed with the recommendations, but maintain that the identified LCCAP-related PPAs were consistent with the Muntinlupa Development Plan and NCCAP. Detailed Matrix was submitted as support. The Management will also ensure adequate funding for the formulation, capacity training, and monitoring and evaluation of climate change-related programs.

Management allocated funds for climate change programs and plans for CY 2023.

An Executive Order signed by the City Mayor includes a representative from CTO as part of MCCB.
observations.reason: During CY 2023, there are still inconsistencies with MCCDP and Muntinlupa Enhanced Climate Change Action Plan (MECCAP).

Ensure that all taxes withheld, employees’ contributions and loan amortizations shall be remitted intact, leaving no variance, and within the reglementary period as provided for by the related law, rules and regulations

Taxes withheld during the year were not conscientiously remitted to the BIR. Moreover, there are still unremitted balances to the concerned government offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Head, Accounting Office to:

adopt the moving average method on the costing of issuances of inventories.

We recommended that the GSO:

Prepare the SSMIs weekly based on the RISs for items actually issued;

Provide the Accounting Office a summary based on the PO and issuances of inventory items in excel format with detailed information similar to the SLCs to facilitate the updating of the SLCs in eNGAS.

Summary with information similar to SLCs submitted as needed.

Last submission of SSMI is for CY 2021.

Per verification, the moving average is part of the eNGAS, but since most of the inventories were not recorded as assets but outright expense, the moving average method was not adopted.

The CAD was not provided with excel format with detailed information, hence, no update on SLCs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Head, Accounting Office to record cost of goods, supplies and materials intended for distribution under the appropriate inventory accounts for distribution and the subsequent distribution/ issuances under the expense accounts based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) prepared by the Supply/Property Officer supported by Requisition Issued Slips (RIS) evidencing receipt by the actual end-users/beneficiaries of goods issued.
observations.reason: Various inventories were recorded as expense outright instead of the corresponding inventory account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
CAD to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the reconciling items amounting to P677,521.34 to reflect the correct cash balance and furnish COA the copy of the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and
CTO to request to the concerned depository banks to furnish the City with the copies of debit and credit memos, if any, on a monthly basis, and submit the same to the CAD for recording in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
CAD to make the necessary adjusting entries for the understatement in the RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT Income accounts and the SET Receivable account and Deferred SET Income accounts amounting to P5,567,723.87 and P39,953,991.24, respectively, to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts based on the submitted RPT and SET Delinquency Listing as of December 31, 2023 and to provide the COA with copies of JEVs upon adjustment; and
CTO to submit yearly, to the CAD, copy furnished the COA with the following reports:
Delinquency Listing for RPT and SET as of December 31 of every year as basis of the CAD in reflecting the correct balances of the affected accounts as at year-end; and
Summary of RPT and SET Receivables for the current year, on the first day of January of every year as basis of the CAD for the accrual of RPT and SET at the beginning of every year.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the -
CAD to immediately record in the books the collections amounting to P365,150.00 and provide the COA with a copy of the corresponding JEV;
OsMun to regularly submit to CAD the necessary documents and reports relative to the claims from PhilHealth and the corresponding collections for proper recording of transactions affecting the Due from GOCCs and other related accounts; and
OsMun to provide the CAD with a copy of prior years’ receivables from PhilHealth, if any, and reconcile the same with the accounting records, thereafter, the CAD to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the same and submit a copy of the corresponding JEV to this Office.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the CTO to revert the existing time deposits of the City to the general fund account since there is no authority of the Sanggunian and approval of the LCE, pursuant to Section 21 of the COA Circular No. 98-382; and
Henceforth, make sure that only idle funds are placed under time deposit account, provided that, there is a specific authority of the Sanggunian and approval of the LCE pursuant to the said Circular.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CAD to maintain the subsidiary ledgers for every accountable officer under the City Treasurer to ensure proper monitoring of accountability in compliance to Sections 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445, and Section 10 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Treasurer require the concerned CO to remit intact daily all collections of PLMun to the CTO, and the latter to deposit the same on a daily basis in pursuant to Section 32, Chapter II of the Revised Cash Examination Manual and Paragraph 1 of Section 69 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Treasurer require each accountable officer of the City to prepare and maintain a cashbook in the prescribed form and to monthly certify therein to ensure proper reconciliation and monitoring of accountabilities pursuant to Item J, No. 1 of RCEM and Section 26, Chapter I, of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the City Treasurer require all accountable officers to provide their safe or vault for proper safekeeping of the cash and cash items pursuant to Item 10, Chapter II of RCEM.

We recommended that the City Treasurer require the CO to increase the bond enough to cover her maximum accountability or to transfer the collections to another CO with sufficient bond to cover the maximum accountability of all collections.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the CAD to prepare and maintain a SL for each NGAs and reconcile the balances reported on the GL and schedules supporting the claims vis-à-vis the SL prepared.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the OsMun and PLMun to submit the required reports to the CAD on quarterly basis, to ensure proper and timely recording of transactions using the accrual method of accounting pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445, and IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the CAD to record purchase of inventories under the corresponding asset account instead of outright expense using the Perpetual Inventory Method as required in     Section 114, of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended the Management to:
Implement immediately the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the City pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.; and
Henceforth, submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of OsMun and CAD to-
Submit the FURs/Liquidation Report, whichever is applicable, and its supporting documents within the prescribed period as indicated in each MOA; and
Ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the FURs and List of Patients to ensure proper screening of beneficiaries and rational use of funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
LSB to fully implement those PPAs indicated in the approved SEF Budget efficiently and effectively pursuant to DEPED-DBM-DILG JC         No. 1, s. 2020 and JC No. 1, s. 2017; and
CED and other concerned personnel to exert effort in conducting the required detailed engineering for capital outlays under SEF on a timely manner and prepare the corresponding documents/reports needed for the procurement and proper implementation of SEF programs and projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the officials concerned to submit copies of the approved contracts/POs totaling 618 amounting to P384,793,555.01 which remain unsubmitted as of this writing, including all supporting documents pursuant to Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of the said Circular.

We recommended that the City Mayor to -
Direct the implementing offices to execute a catch-up plan for the 50 partially and unimplemented LCCAP-related PPAs; and
Henceforth, properly monitor the efficient and full implementation of the identified PPAs to ensure the achievement of the City in improving its adaptive capacity and reduced the adverse impacts of climate change; and

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the MDDRMO to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP and submit the same to the Sangguniang Panglungsod for the enactment of appropriation ordinance indicating the PPAs to be charged against the STF before making any disbursements.

We recommended that the City Mayor -
Require the CTO to remit the FSIF to the BFP within the stipulated period stated in the MOA and to revisit the existing MOA by and between the City and BFP, by including clear provisions as to the remittance 80% FSIF, the retention of 20% share of the City pursuant to Section 13 of RA No. 9514, and other lacking provisions relative thereto; and
Direct the CTO and CAD to strictly monitor the request for collection of the 20% share of the City pursuant to Section 13 of RA No. 9514.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CAD to –
Remit in full the withheld taxes of P4,435,665.31 to the BIR; and
Henceforth, remit the succeeding taxes withheld in full, within ten (10) days following the month in which the withholding was made to avoid unnecessary payment of penalties, interest and/or surcharges pursuant to RMC No. 23-2007 and Section 247 (b), Chapter I of the NIRC.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
CAD to remit in full the unremitted mandatory contributions and payment of employee’s loan amortization to the concerned government agencies; and remittance should be made within the reglementary period in compliance with RA Nos. 8291 and 10606, and HDMF Circular No. 275; and
CHRMD to require timely application and/or registration to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG of the newly hired employees as well as update the employment status of the promoted and retired personnel.

We recommended the management to:
Require the City Accountant to issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books which shall be one of the basis before granting another cash advance to the SDO; and

Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Strict monitoring of liquidation of advances and constant reminder to overdue accounts.



The City is complying with the provisions under COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to-

Require the Finance Department of the PLMUN to attach and submit the pre-signed payrolls and other supporting documents for a particular pay period prior to the grant of cash advances, to ascertain its legality and propriety;

Consider assigning a separate Accountable Officer/SDO from PLMUN exclusively to handle cash advances for payroll of PLMUN’s employees; and

Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of PD No. 1445 and Sections 4.2.1 and 4.2.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Copy of payrolls are attached to grants and liquidation of cash advances for payroll.


Management assigned an AO/SDO to exclusively handle cash advances of PLMUN as evident by an Office Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management to require the City Accountant to record only as payables those procured goods and services that had been actually delivered and accepted or rendered as of the cut-off period pursuant to Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Payables are only recognized at year-end once there is actual delivery and acceptance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to –

Ensure PPAs lined-up for implementation during the year are fully carried out as planned, therefore, the benefits and services envisioned for GAD-related PPAs are continuously enjoyed and/or availed by the constituents; and

Henceforth, prepare and submit all the necessary reports for proper planning, monitoring, and evaluation of GAD programs to the DILG Regional Office and strictly comply with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction:
The Management committed to implement the provisions of existing laws, rules and regulations in addressing gender and development issues and concerns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the City Engineering Department to provide justification for the inclusion of cost/charges in the computation of ABC for the ten infrastructure projects, and henceforth, to be cautious in the preparation of the ABC in accordance with Sections 5, 17.2 and 18 of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and DPWH DO No. 197, s. 2016.
observations.managementAction: Computation of ABC is based on the DPWH DO No. 143, s. 2017. They also highlighted that even if some items identified might be construed as non-civil works, the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 allows for mixed procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management to require the BAC, including the Technical Working Group to submit justification on the excessive ABC, and henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 31 and 32.2.1b of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The submitted justification from Management were acceptable and satisfactory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to require the BAC and the City Engineering Department to submit immediately the documents for further verification and audit, and henceforth, to strictly comply with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The supporting document for contracts were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to stop the simulated transfer of cash to ensure that the unutilized QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE balances from previous year/s are recorded using the appropriate accounts to avoid misstatements of affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The CAD have reconciled the STF account and subsequently transfer the unexpended balance to the LDRRMF-STF with physical transfer of cash.   The corresponding JEVs were likewise prepared to record the said transfer.

Properly plan the implementation of all PPAs funded out of the 20% DF to avoid delays and ensure 100% accomplishment within a particular period

Henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

The guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the DF were properly observed.

observations.managementAction: The CED, together with the concerned offices provide proper planning in identifying priority projects of the City.

Develop mechanisms for lessons learned to feed back into the LGU organizational learning, decision-making, DRRM planning, implementation, and monitoring and evaluation; and

Furnish the CAO the monitoring and evaluation reports after the same have been approved.

observations.reason:

Per validation, no separate SLs by year for the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and the related deferred accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the:
City Treasurer to make a formal request to the concerned depository banks to furnish the City with the copies of CMs/DMs pertaining to the reconciling items appearing in the monthly bank statements; and
Head, Accounting Office to analyze the reconciling items pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996, and make the necessary adjusting entries in the accounting books, if warranted, and provide this Office copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) relative thereto.

We recommended the Management to require the Accounting Office and Treasury Office to:
Effect the immediate actual transfers of P72,325,748.00; and
Stop the simulated transfer of cash to ensure that the unutilized QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE balances from previous year/s are recorded using the appropriate accounts to avoid misstatements of affected accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly observe the allocation of 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRM to support disaster risk management activities pursuant to Section 21 of RA No. 10121;
Require the DRRMO to prepare and submit to COA copies of revised/supplemental AIP for LDRRMF or the LDRRMFIP on every realignment of LDRRM PPAs at a particular period; and
Henceforth, set targets on highly prioritized PPAs for LDRRM and closely monitor the implementation of the same to achieve maximum/full utilization of allocated funds.

We recommended that Management to require the Engineering Office to:
Prepare a calibrated catch-up plan to expedite/fast-track the completion of the 10 infrastructure projects;
Impose liquidated damages on Contractors based on the applicable rate on the cost of unperformed portion of the contract for every day of delay to enforce project completion on time; and
Henceforth, ensure that detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs, including right of way, for the project have been sufficiently undertaken before bidding and award of contract to prevent incurrence of delays in the project completion and contract implementation.

We recommended that Management to:
Kindly require the concerned personnel to submit explanations why there are delays in the submission of approved government contracts and P.O.s together with its supporting documents and the Notices of Delivery within the prescribed timelines;
Henceforth, to require the Head of the following offices to:
Head, BAC Secretariat to ensure 100% submission of approved government contracts and P.O.s with complete supporting documents within five days from approval of the same and ensure completeness of information in the P.O.s pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Head, GSO to adhere strictly with the notification of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Chairperson to reassess the programs, activities, and projects of the City using the Harmonized GAD Guidelines in order to allocate at least five (5) percent of the total annual budget of the City pursuant to the PCW-NEDA-DBM JC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to analyze the Due to BIR account and immediately remit the amount due to BIR; and
Moreover, ensure prompt and full remittance of all taxes deducted and withheld pursuant to BIR Regulation No. 2-98, moving forward.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to analyze the unremitted deductions and immediately remit the amounts due to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG.
Likewise, strictly observe the remittance of contributions within the prescribed deadlines.
We recommended that the Management immediately enforce the settlement of all suspensions and disallowances in compliance with Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:

The MCCPPEID to –

Check from OCD the available criteria/guidelines for the accreditation of the volunteer groups; and
Undertake the accreditation process of the various volunteer groups, and submit the lists of the volunteers to the OCD for inclusion in the database of the community disaster volunteers.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, CDRRMO to coordinate with the concerned City Officials for the immediate implementation of PPAs and utilization of the LDRRMF to achieve its purpose of reducing and mitigating the impact of calamities and disaster that may occur in the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor advice the LSB Co-Chairman to maximize the utilization of the SEF, specifically those unimplemented projects, out of the continuing appropriations and strictly monitor its implementation to address the actual needs of the schools.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Budget Officer (CBO) to:
Refrain from certifying as to availability of funds when there is no sufficient appropriation; and
Ensure that the appropriation for Personal Services is computed correctly based on the actual number of personnel to avoid overdraft.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the LCPC Council to:
Prioritize the adoption of the LDP for Children as well as the Comprehensive Juvenile Intervention Program which are among the mandatory functions of the LCPC; and
Implement in full the PPAs for LCPC in order to achieve its basic purpose of protecting the rights and welfare of the children in conflict with the law.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to transfer the amount of P193,303,695.51 to the DF bank account to ensure the availability of cash for  utilization on priority development projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor cause the strict implementation of all the programs and activities under the GPB to achieve the overall objective of the GAD programs.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, DA to:
Ensure that all the necessary arrangements preliminary to the implementation of the project have been undertaken;
Prepare immediately the MOA and Project Proposal after the approval of the proper location by the concerned national agencies; and
Prepare the required monthly monitoring report.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Market Administrator to review the lessees’ compliance with the conditions of the previous contract, and thereafter, recommend the renewal of contracts for qualified lessees.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Market Administrator to execute new Contracts of Lease to the qualified applicants and to collect the goodwill money from the awardees of the stalls before the issuance of the Certificate of Award/Occupancy.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Market Administrator to impose and collect the 25 percent surcharges on the total rent due for the late payment of daily rentals for a total of 15 days or more, as provided under Section 13 and Item B, Section 22 of the Market Code.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Market Administrator to inspect and validate actual occupants of the stalls to determine if they were awardees of the contracts of lease, otherwise, cause the non-renewal or revocation of the contracts of lease issued to original awardees, and the eviction of sub-lessees.

We recommended that the Market Administrator of the MCPM:
Inspect, investigate and monitor the existing stallholders and their sub-lessees;
Cause the termination of the lease of those tenants with more than two stalls in the market premises; and
Ensure that no person be allowed to lease more than two stalls in the market premises.

We recommended that the Committee on Award/Adjudication of stalls submit to the Market Committee the inventory of vacant stalls for their approval, and post the Notice of Vacancy for a period of not less than 
10 days prior to the date of award of the available stalls.

We recommended that the Head, Accounting Office account for the discrepancies noted in the above accounts and ensure that taxes withheld and mandatory premium contributions and loan payments collected from employees are remitted promptly and in full amount to the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
Head, Engineering Office to:
Conduct the inspection of these structures to determine the current status and its existence;
Conduct appraisal of these structures which may be the basis of recording if documents to support the project cost could no longer be found; and
Head, Accounting Office to make adjustments in the books as necessary after reconciliation and analysis.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Accounting Office to compute for the depreciation of the above listed PPE and to take up the necessary adjustments to correct the account balances.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to maintain Property Cards by class/type of PPE as required under MNGAS, to facilitate reconciliation with the RPCPPE and the accounting records.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
Head, Engineering Office and City Accounting Office to reconcile all on-going projects. Both should locate the files and documents including the final inspections and acceptance of the CIP projects, validate and evaluate the projects, and  submit its report on the status of the projects; and
City Accounting Office to update its subsidiary records supporting the transactions booked as completed, and those CIPs for purpose of ascertaining the existence and propriety of accepted projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
City Treasurer to:
Submit daily to the Accounting Office the Report of Check Issued, together with the paid DVs and supporting documents;
Follow-up from all depository banks the submission of the complete copies of credit memos (CMs) and debit memos (DMs) per month as well as prior years’ CMs/DMs to the City;
Prepare the cashbook for all bank accounts, which shall be updated and balanced daily and at the end of the month or when required to do so by proper competent authority.
Head, Accounting Office to:
Inform the City Treasurer’s Office of the presence of unrecorded disbursements, CMs and DMs in the Bank Reconciliation Statements; and
Analyze the discrepancies between the book and bank balances, and effect adjustments as necessary.
City Treasurer and the Head, Accounting Office to conduct monthly reconciliation of the SL and cashbook balances to ensure the correctness and completeness of the recorded entries.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
City Treasurer and the Head, Accounting Office to conduct monthly reconciliation of the SL and cashbook balances to ensure the correctness and completeness of the recorded entries; and
City Treasurer to prepare the cashbook for all bank accounts, which shall be updated and balanced daily and at the end of the month or when required to do so by proper competent authority.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to transfer the accounts maintained with PNB to an AGDB, otherwise, the account could be maintained only upon the approval from the BLGF.
We recommended that the Head, Accounting Office record the accrued expenses at year-end to establish the correct amount of expenses and payables in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local School Board to:
Stop paying honoraria and allowances of Madrasah asatidz and ALS instructional managers and coordinators out of SEF;
Ensure that expenditures to be charged against the SEF are among the allowable expenditures as prescribed under pertinent laws, rules and regulations; and
Require the concerned DepEd official to refund the P0.033 million due to unauthorized travel expenses to Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia; otherwise the amount will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
City Treasurer and the disbursing officer to ensure that payrolls are signed upon payment and that authorization letter is required from persons claiming other than the payee;
Head, Procurement Office and the Local School Board to refrain from the procurement of supplies and materials through cash advances and observe the proper process of procurement and use of the appropriate procurement method under the circumstances; and
Head, Accounting Office to ensure that all claims are supported with complete and relevant documents before the processing of payment.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the MCPC to:
Ensure the full implementation of planned PPAs for children; and
Prioritize the formulation of the LCPC and LIPC as well as the development of a monitoring and evaluation assessment tools for the PPAs to provide the basis for subsequent PPAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor advise the MCPC to undertake the monitoring of PPAs to ensure their timely implementation and completion, for immediate delivery of services and benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the LMO and Social Service Department to take immediate action to ensure immediate implementation of the projects to avoid further delay.

We recommended that the City Mayor coordinate with the Heads of the LMO and DA for the early resolution of the issues hindering the implementation of the Fishery Project under the BUB to avoid further delay.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Engineering Office and UPAO to strictly monitor the implementation of the contract to avoid further delays in the Construction of the Micro-Medium-Rise Buildings so that the 200 household/beneficiaries could immediately occupy the housing units.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Social Service Department to submit the required documents and comply with the guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.

We recommended that Management require the:
MESWMB to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003 in the establishment of MRFs for the barangays;
Barangays to prioritize identifying lots in order to establish and operate their own MRFs; and
Head, Environmental Sanitation Center to remit the proceeds from sale of recyclables from their own MRF.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Engineering Office to:
Ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction have been addressed; and
Review thoroughly the request for work suspensions by the contractors based on complete and relevant documents. .

We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to:
Comply strictly with the documentation of each procurement phase to ensure availability of complete documents after contract signing to preclude the deficiencies noted; and
Submit the complete required documents within the timelines to the Office of the City Auditor.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Treasurer to impose and collect the additional ad valorem tax on the owners or persons having interest on  idle lands annually; and
City Assessor to prepare and establish an accurate information as to the taxable and exempted idle properties.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning Officer and the City Budget Officer to identify in the AIP the specific PPAs, implementing office, implementation schedule, expected outputs, performance indicators, funding source, and the costs to ensure that there are no lump sum appropriations in the City’s subsequent Executive Budget.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Budget Officer to:
Monitor all unexpended balances of the DF and  determine unexpended balances of the DF to ensure that the same are re-appropriated for allowable development projects; and
Re-appropriate the unexpended balances of appropriations for CYs 2014 to 2017 under the DF for eligible development projects.

We recommended that City Mayor maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various PPAs that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
City Treasurer  to request the LBP to transfer 20% DF of the gross IRA to the DF bank account including prior years’ unexpended balances to ensure the availability of cash to the extent of the Fund’s appropriation and its utilization for priority development projects; and
Head, Accounting Office to reconcile the balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA of the 20% DF and GF-Proper accounts to arrive at the correct balance of the accounts and make the necessary cash transfers to the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC, CMDRRMO to:
Ensure that the 30 percent QRF is provided in the LDRRMFIP as a lump sum appropriation to allow its immediate use in times if calamity and disaster; and
Evaluate the need to use the STF and to indicate the specific planned PPAs in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
City Budget Officer to revert the unexpended balance of continuing appropriation of P32.041 million to the  unappropriated surplus of the GF and to have the same re-appropriated for the LDRRMF in the succeeding Executive Budget; and
CBMO and the Accounting Office to reconcile the amount of the continuing appropriation, utilization and balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
Head, Accounting Office to   ensure that the unexpended QRF and MF balances transferred to the STF are recorded using the appropriate accounts to preclude misstatements of the affected accounts;  and
City Treasurer to transfer immediately the actual cash of P81.764 million to the STF.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Accounting Office to stop the practice of reimbursement and instruct all concerned officials and employees to observe the prescribed process of procurement.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, GAD Office to:
Undertake the HGDG assessment to provide the bases for the attribution of budgets for PPAs to the GAD budget;
Document the results thereof and determine the percentage of the GAD-related PPAs’ budgets which could be attributed to the GAD budget and reflect the same in the GPB; and
Prepare the GPB and GAD AR in accordance with JMC 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, GAD Office to coordinate with the CBMO to see to it that the GPB and AEB are reconciled.
We recommended that the City implement strictly or in full all the programs and activities under the GPB to achieve the overall objective of the GAD programs.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Head, Accounting Office to stop the practice of reimbursement.  We also recommended to instruct all concerned officials and employees to henceforth, observe the prescribed process of procurement.

We recommended that City Mayor require the Head, Accounting Office to thoroughly review transactions to ensure the correctness of accounting entries made and the accuracy and reliability of the related account balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Treasurer to:
Adopt plans/strategies in order to attain the estimated revenue for CY 2018;
Adopt measures and strategies on how to improve collection efficiency particularly in collecting Real Property Taxes; and
b. Local Finance Committee to prepare a realistic target for revenues to be realized from Real Properties taking into consideration the statistic of previous years’ collection.

We recommended that the Head, Accounting Office ensure prompt and full remittance of taxes withheld from the employees and the City Government’s contributions to the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to preclude the imposition of interest and penalties and the limit in the access to services and benefits by the City Government.
We also recommended that the Head, Accounting Office prior years’ unremitted balances and immediately remit the amount due.
